mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 5.5% indicates tightening labor market conditions, which historically correlate with stronger corporate earnings growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 5.5% fall below 0.5% on average, or the correlation between UNRATE and SPX returns drops below 0.25.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and housing starts (HOUST) signals robust supply-side expansion, which drives corporate profitability and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint 20-day return of INDPRO and HOUST declines by more than 15% simultaneously, or the interaction term's coefficient in a regression model falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate above 4.5% during high inflationary environments (CPIAUCSL > 3%) triggers monetary tightening, which historically leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 3% exceed 2% on average, or the interaction term's p-value exceeds 0.1 in a regression model.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) above 200k monthly jobs indicates sustained labor market strength, which drives consumer spending and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in periods with PAYEMS > 200k fall below 0.3% on average, or the correlation between PAYEMS and SPX returns drops below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth (GDPC1) above 3% signals strong aggregate demand, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with GDPC1 > 3% fall below 1.5% on average, or the regression coefficient for GDPC1 drops below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising consumer spending (RSAFS) and declining housing starts (HOUST) indicates shifting demand patterns, which creates volatility and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between RSAFS and HOUST shows a positive coefficient in 60% of cases, or SPX 20-day returns in these scenarios exceed 1% on average.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average during rising oil prices (CPIAUCSL > 2%) signals supply-side constraints, which historically lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in periods with INDPRO < 12mo MA and CPIAUCSL > 2% exceed 1.5% on average, or the interaction term's p-value exceeds 0.05.
